Apple closed at roughly $4.88 trillion as its shares held steady, while Nvidia fell 3.5% to approximately $4.86 trillion. Nvidia had held the top spot for nearly a year after becoming the first company to surpass […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Apple is in early settlement talks with the DOJ over its iPhone antitrust case https://thenextweb.com/news/apple-doj-antitrust-settlement-talks-iphone Fri, 17 Jul 2026 19:14:31 +0000 TheNextWeb=fc51f2e0c8bb73043f7ae93ff3cd20c1 Apple and the US Department of Justice are in early discussions about settling the 2024 antitrust lawsuit that alleges Apple violated competition law through its iPhone ecosystem, Bloomberg reported on Thursday. Apple has made multiple offers this year to close the case. The discussions are active but there is no guarantee of an agreement, and […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Tesla made a $225 balance bike for toddlers. It has no motor, no pedals, and is already sold out. https://thenextweb.com/news/tesla-balance-bike-kids-225-sold-out Fri, 17 Jul 2026 18:55:54 +0000 TheNextWeb=e97c0441208f7b61e112ee1b8882f143 Tesla launched a $225 balance bike for children aged 2 to 5. It has no motor, no pedals, no brakes, and is powered entirely by toddler legs. The lightweight white magnesium frame comes with a five-way adjustable seat, the Tesla wordmark on the side, and the T logo on the front. It sold out immediately, […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> Microsoft will stop syncing OneDrive on older Windows 10 versions next month https://thenextweb.com/news/microsoft-onedrive-drops-old-windows-10-sync Fri, 17 Jul 2026 18:36:29 +0000 TheNextWeb=205df52e896dd1b7bffb56272bca5fcd Microsoft will end OneDrive synchronisation support for Windows 10 versions before 22H2 on August 15. After that date, users on older builds will receive no more sync app updates, bug fixes, or security patches. The service itself will not immediately stop working, but Microsoft says “future functionality is not guaranteed.” Windows 10 22H2, the final […] This story continues at The Next Web]]> An AWS billing bug sent users estimated charges of up to $2.5 trillion https://thenextweb.com/news/aws-billing-bug-billion-dollar-estimates Fri, 17 Jul 2026 18:21:09 +0000 TheNextWeb=f2c5e34516e4f09e004b65620d3462a1 AWS users woke up Friday to find estimated billing emails showing charges ranging from hundreds of millions to $2.5 trillion. A unit pricing error in the AWS Billing Console’s estimated billing computation subsystem was the cause, Amazon confirmed. One Reddit user whose charges totalled $0.19 last month received an estimated bill of nearly $2.5 billion. CNBC confirmed the early-stage discussions. The talks come weeks after Anthropic announced a $1.25 billion-per-month arrangement with SpaceX to use the Colossus 1 data centre’s Nvidia GPUs.
